text: The food was fresh, flavorful, and served hot. The staff was friendly and made the whole visit enjoyable.

The pictures they display are far from what they serve. Frozen breaded chicken strips, tomato paste concentrate, and bitter salad leaves are a few of the quality drops.
I ordered a Caesar salad and there is more green on Mars than there was on my Salad. I was served all the yellow bitter salad leaves.

Also the pizza arrive around 6:20pm the sub that was ordered at the same time didn't arrive till after 7pm.
This was my favorite restaurant. Since the interior remodel, the taste of the food and the service has dropped to below fast food chain quality, disappointed.

I was driving home and passed Pizzaioli Italian Cuisine and decided to give it a try since I’d never been there before. It looks like one of those hole-in-the-wall places that could be a hidden gem… unfortunately, that wasn’t the case.
When I walked in, there was one lady ordering at the register and no hostess or sign explaining how things worked. Since it was my first time and there was only one person dining in, I assumed you order at the counter. I told the employee working the register that I’d never been there before and asked for a menu. I ordered a chicken parm sub and sat myself at a table to dine in.
While I was waiting, the employee came over and said my to-go order was almost ready. I let her know I was planning to eat there and didn’t realize it had been put in as a to-go order. This was never mentioned or asked when I ordered, even after I specifically said it was my first time there. I was then told I wasn’t allowed to dine in because the order had already been put in as to-go and that it was “the boss’s rule.” I had even left a tip when I ordered.
So I was essentially forced to sit in my car and eat my food. To make matters worse, the chicken parm sub was burnt to a crisp and barely edible.
Overall, it was just a terrible experience from start to finish. Confusing service, poor customer handling, and bad food. I will not be returning. Picture of my chicken parm sandwich(taken from my car since i was forced to eat there) below
